conflict.title: Conflict Intelligence™
conflict.subtitle: Predict. Prevent. Resolve.
conflict.p1: Built on the Harvard Negotiation Project’s interest-based negotiation framework — the methodology behind Getting to Yes (Fisher, Ury & Patton) and Difficult Conversations (Stone, Patton & Heen). Every layer of the module is designed to surface underlying interests, not just manage surface-level positions.
conflict.p2: The AI analysis maps anonymous survey data to the Three Conversations framework: the “What Happened” conversation (divergent narratives), the Feelings conversation (suppressed emotions driving escalation), and the Identity conversation (threats to self-image causing rigidity). This multi-layer diagnosis enables interventions that address the full depth of conflict.
conflict.p3: Manager conversation guides translate principled negotiation into practical scripts any manager can use — with interest-probing questions, emotion-acknowledging language, and collaborative resolution pathways. When bilateral resolution isn’t enough, the structured escalation pathway activates William Ury’s “Third Side” model for professional mediation.
conflict.wf1Desc: Anonymous pulse & deep-dive surveys detect early conflict signals across teams
conflict.wf2Desc: Pattern recognition scores risk (0–100) and identifies conflict types with root causes
conflict.wf3Desc: Manager conversation scripts and recommended actions across time horizons
conflict.wf4Desc: Track actions to completion or escalate to professional mediation
conflict.f1: Bi-weekly pulse (15 items) & quarterly deep-dive (21 items)
conflict.f2: AI risk scoring with low/medium/high/critical levels
conflict.f5: Prioritised action plans (immediate / short / long term)

coaching.title: Coaching & Development
coaching.subtitle: From Engagement to Transformation.
coaching.p1: A complete coaching practice management platform built on the GROW Model (Goal, Reality, Options, Will) and aligned with ICF Core Competencies. Coaches manage the full engagement lifecycle — from discovery and chemistry matching through structured sessions to final assessment and alumni follow-up.
coaching.p2: Every session is enriched with AI-powered documentation, pre/post session intake forms, and EQ-i 2.0 psychometric integration (5 composites, 15 subscales). Coaches can generate AI-driven debrief narratives from assessment data, automatically populate session plans, and track coaching arcs across engagements.
coaching.p3: Sponsor organisations get transparent reporting on engagement progress, session completion rates, and development outcomes — with full contract management, invoicing, and rebillable session tracking built in. The coachee portal provides a dedicated space for session booking, between-session reflection, and progress visibility.
coaching.wf1Desc: Intake assessment, contracting, and baseline psychometric evaluation
coaching.wf2Desc: GROW Model IDP creation with AI-generated development plans
coaching.wf3Desc: Pre/post forms, AI notes, ratings, and coaching arc tracking
coaching.wf4Desc: Sponsor reporting, progress measurement, and alumni follow-up
coaching.f1: GROW Model session framework with AI documentation
coaching.f3: Pre/post session intake forms linked to templates
coaching.f6: Coachee portal for booking and between-session work
coaching.f7: Coach journal with AI-generated session summaries

neuro.title: Neuro-Inclusion
neuro.subtitle: Build a Truly Inclusive Workplace.
neuro.p1: Assess your organisation’s neuroinclusion maturity across multiple dimensions with structured assessments designed for neurodivergent employees and the teams that support them. The module moves beyond awareness training to measurable, actionable inclusion strategies.
neuro.p2: AI-driven gap analysis benchmarks your scores against dimension-level targets, identifies the highest-impact areas for improvement, and generates targeted action plans. Progress is tracked over time, allowing organisations to demonstrate measurable inclusion gains to leadership, boards, and external stakeholders.
neuro.p3: The assessment covers physical environment, communication practices, management approaches, recruitment processes, career development pathways, and organisational culture — providing a holistic view of where neurodivergent employees experience friction and where the organisation excels.
neuro.wf2Desc: AI benchmarks scores and identifies highest-impact gaps
neuro.wf3Desc: Targeted inclusion action plans per dimension
neuro.wf4Desc: Measure progress over time with repeat assessments
neuro.f8: Integration with coaching for manager development

leadership.title: Leadership & Succession
leadership.subtitle: Develop Tomorrow’s Leaders Today.
leadership.p1: Build a measurable leadership pipeline with AI-generated Individual Development Plans using the GROW coaching model. The module synthesises inputs from EQ-i 2.0 assessments, conflict analysis data, competency gap evaluations, and coach observations to create evidence-based development roadmaps.
leadership.p2: Each IDP includes structured goals, reality assessments, options for development, and concrete action commitments — with milestones that can be tracked to completion. The AI doesn’t just generate a plan; it contextualises development priorities against the individual’s assessment profile and organisational needs.
leadership.p3: The conflict-to-IDP pipeline is unique: when the Conflict Intelligence module identifies patterns that suggest a leadership development need, the system can generate a targeted IDP that addresses the root cause — turning conflict data into growth opportunities.
leadership.wf1Desc: EQ-i 2.0, competency evaluations, and conflict data inputs
leadership.wf2Desc: AI creates GROW Model IDP with contextualised priorities
leadership.wf3Desc: Track milestone completion with notes and evidence
leadership.wf4Desc: Regenerate plans as new data emerges from coaching & assessments
leadership.f5: Conflict-to-IDP pipeline for root cause development
leadership.f7: Plan regeneration as new data arrives

booking.title: Booking & Scheduling
booking.subtitle: Effortless Scheduling, Zero Friction.
booking.p1: Professional booking pages for every coach with real-time availability, timezone-aware slot generation, and configurable rules for buffer times, advance notice, and daily limits. Clients access a public, shareable link — no login required — and book in seconds.
booking.p2: Google Calendar two-way sync means bookings automatically create calendar events with Google Meet links, and changes in either direction (reschedule, cancel, decline) propagate instantly via webhook. Date exclusions, country holidays, and special availability rules ensure coaches maintain full control over their schedule.
booking.p3: Built natively into ARTES — no Calendly dependency, no redirect to third-party tools. All booking data is scoped by coach and tenant, with automated confirmation and reminder emails via AWS SES keeping both parties informed.
booking.wf1Desc: Set weekly availability, event types, timezone, and buffer rules
booking.wf2Desc: Public booking link — no login required for clients
booking.wf3Desc: Client selects slot; calendar event + Meet link auto-created
booking.wf4Desc: Two-way Google Calendar sync with webhook-driven updates
booking.f1: Public shareable booking pages per coach
booking.f2: Google Calendar two-way sync with webhooks
booking.f5: Configurable buffer time and advance notice

intake.title: Intake Management
intake.subtitle: One Engine, Every Assessment.
intake.p1: A flexible intake engine powering every module in the platform. Design custom questionnaires with mixed question types (scale, boolean, text, multi-select), deploy them as anonymous self-service surveys, coach-led interviews, or structured assessments — all from a single template builder.
intake.p2: Privacy-first aggregation ensures that individual responses are never exposed. A minimum group size of 5 respondents per analysis prevents statistical de-anonymisation, while public survey links allow distribution without requiring respondents to create accounts. Coach-led sessions capture richer contextual data with session format, target name, and coachee linkage.
intake.p3: Cross-module analytics feed survey data into Conflict Intelligence (risk scoring), Neuro-Inclusion (maturity assessment), and Coaching (pre/post session forms) — making the intake engine the connective tissue of the entire platform.
intake.wf1Desc: Build templates with mixed question types and branching logic
intake.wf2Desc: Anonymous links, coach-led sessions, or embedded assessments
intake.wf3Desc: Privacy-first response aggregation with minimum group sizes
intake.wf4Desc: Feed data into Conflict, Neuro-Inclusion, and Coaching modules
intake.f1: Custom template builder with mixed question types
intake.f2: Anonymous self-service surveys via public links

CONFLICT.title: Conflict Intelligence™
CONFLICT.subtitle: Proactive workplace conflict detection and resolution grounded in Helena's coaching-integrated mediation methodology
CONFLICT.coreInsight: Core Insight: Most workplace conflict is predictable. Warning signs appear weeks or months before a formal complaint is filed. Conflict Intelligence makes those signals visible — and actionable — for leaders who lack the training to see them.
CONFLICT.mediationPathwayDesc: When coaching guides are insufficient, escalate directly to Helena's professional mediation services — coaching-integrated, interest-based, and designed to preserve relationships.
CONFLICT.step1Desc: Click "Escalate" on any high or critical risk analysis. HR is notified immediately.
CONFLICT.step2Desc: Helena reviews the AI analysis and conducts a 30-min intake with the HR contact to understand context.
CONFLICT.step3Desc: Interest-based negotiation session facilitated by Helena — focuses on underlying needs, not positions.
CONFLICT.step4Desc: Agreement documented, follow-up pulse survey scheduled to verify resolution at 30 and 60 days.
CONFLICT.drillHint: Run a focused sub-analysis for each detected conflict type.
CONFLICT.professionalReviewEmpty: Escalate the analysis to a professional to enable this section. Use the button in Recommended Actions.
CONFLICT.reviewNotesPlaceholder: Document your independent assessment of the conflict situation…
CONFLICT.awaitingReview: The assigned professional has not yet submitted their review.
CONFLICT.generateIntakeTooltip: Auto-generate a follow-up intake survey based on this action item
CONFLICT.viewGeneratedIntake: Open the generated intake in Intake Management
CONFLICT.escalationRequested: Escalation has been requested — HR / Coach has been notified.
CONFLICT.noTemplatesWarn: No conflict intake templates found. Create a intake template first, collect at least 5 responses, then run an analysis.
CONFLICT.analysisTitlePlaceholder: e.g. Q1 2026 Team Health, March Pulse
CONFLICT.analysisTitleHint: Give this analysis a name for easy reference
CONFLICT.privacyMinResponses: Analysis requires a minimum of {{count}} responses to protect individual privacy. Results are aggregated — no individual data is shown.
CONFLICT.generateActionsDesc: Generate concrete, prioritized action items based on this analysis. Actions are grouped by timeframe — immediate, short-term, and long-term.
CONFLICT.skillDevDesc: Generate AI-powered Individual Development Plans (IDPs) based on conflict analysis findings — targeted skill building using the GROW model.
CONFLICT.noIDPsYet: No conflict-based development plans yet. Run an analysis first, then generate a targeted IDP for team members.
CONFLICT.idpDialogDesc: Create a targeted IDP based on a conflict analysis. The AI will generate a GROW-model development plan focused on the conflict types and risk patterns identified.
CONFLICT.selectAnalysisHint: Select the conflict analysis to base this plan on
CONFLICT.teamMemberHint: Team member who will follow the development plan
CONFLICT.developmentGoalsPlaceholder: e.g. Improve conflict resolution skills, develop active listening, learn interest-based negotiation techniques
CONFLICT.developmentGoalsHint: Describe the key skills or behaviours to develop
CONFLICT.generateIDPFailed: Failed to generate IDP. Please try again.
CONFLICT.knowledgeSkillBuildingDesc: Structured learning paths to build conflict literacy, emotional intelligence, and leadership capability — drawing on tools available within this platform and leading external assessments.
CONFLICT.negotiationToolkitDesc: Downloadable frameworks and guided exercises for self-directed conflict resolution, based on Helena's methodology.
CONFLICT.neuroInclusionDesc: Identify neuroinclusion gaps that often underlie perceived conflict — communication style mismatches, sensory overload, and cognitive diversity barriers.
CONFLICT.leadershipIDPDesc: Build individual development plans using the GROW coaching model to strengthen self-awareness and conflict-resilient leadership behaviours.
CONFLICT.coachInterviewDesc: Conduct structured one-to-one or group intake interviews to surface unspoken tensions before they escalate.
CONFLICT.tkiDesc: Identify your default conflict handling mode — competing, collaborating, compromising, avoiding, or accommodating — and learn when each is most effective.
CONFLICT.eqi20Desc: The world's leading emotional intelligence assessment. Measure self-awareness, empathy, and stress tolerance.
CONFLICT.eq360Desc: Multi-rater emotional intelligence feedback to reveal blind spots and strengthen leadership effectiveness.
CONFLICT.discDesc: Understand behavioural styles (Dominance, Influence, Steadiness, Conscientiousness) that shape communication and conflict dynamics.
CONFLICT.mbtiDesc: Understand personality type differences that drive communication friction and team conflict.
CONFLICT.viaDesc: Free evidence-based strengths profiling. Reframe conflict conversations around what each person brings.
CONFLICT.positionsFrameworkDesc: Identify underlying needs behind stated positions to find creative solutions both parties can accept.
CONFLICT.interestMappingDesc: Guided exercise to map each party's interests before entering a difficult conversation.
CONFLICT.batnaGuideDesc: Structured evaluation of your Best Alternative To a Negotiated Agreement — know your walk-away point before entering the conversation.
CONFLICT.reframingExercisesDesc: Practice converting positional statements into interest-based questions using the Harvard method.
CONFLICT.conflictDiagnosticDesc: Determine whether conflict is interpersonal, structural, cultural, or positional to choose the right intervention.
CONFLICT.managerPlannerDesc: Step-by-step guide for preparing and facilitating a conflict conversation using interest-based negotiation principles.
CONFLICT.balconyTechniqueDesc: William Ury's guided exercise for stepping back from reactive emotions to gain perspective before engaging in a difficult conversation.
CONFLICT.drillHintText: Run a focused sub-analysis for each detected conflict type to get deeper insights and targeted manager scripts.
CONFLICT.escalationBanner: Escalation has been requested — HR / Coach has been notified.
CONFLICT.idpDialogDescText: Create a targeted IDP based on a conflict analysis. The AI will generate a GROW-model development plan focused on the conflict types and risk patterns identified.
CONFLICT.generateIDPFailedMsg: Failed to generate IDP. Please try again.

NEURO.title: Neuro-Inclusion Compass™
NEURO.subtitle: Assess, build, and sustain neuroinclusive cultures — organizational diagnostic across 8 evidence-based dimensions
NEURO.marketTiming: Market Timing: Neurodiversity-affirming workplace practices are transitioning from a values statement to a legal requirement across Canada and the US. The Neuro-Inclusion Compass provides the most differentiated tool for mid-market organizations — evidence-based, action-oriented, and grounded in Helena's signature methodology.
NEURO.rateOrg: Rate your organization on a scale of 0–100
NEURO.dimAwarenessCultureDesc: Organization-wide understanding and genuine appreciation of neurodiversity — from leadership commitment to team-level norms
NEURO.dimRecruitmentOnboardingDesc: Inclusive hiring practices, accessible job postings, alternative interview formats, and structured onboarding for neurodivergent hires
NEURO.dimWorkspaceEnvironmentDesc: Physical accommodations: sensory-friendly spaces, quiet zones, flexible seating, and lighting adjustments
NEURO.dimCommunicationCollaborationDesc: Flexible communication styles, written vs. verbal options, meeting structure, and asynchronous work support
NEURO.dimLeadershipManagementDesc: Manager capability to recognize, support, and advocate for neurodivergent employees — coaching skills and individualized approaches
NEURO.dimPolicyAccommodationDesc: Formal policies supporting neuroinclusion, clear accommodation request pathways, and legally compliant response processes
NEURO.dimLearningDevelopmentDesc: Accessible training content, multiple learning modalities, and growth opportunities that do not disadvantage neurodivergent employees
NEURO.dimWorkflowTaskStructureDesc: How work is structured, sequenced, and communicated — supporting executive function, reducing ambiguity, and enabling deep focus
NEURO.accommodationDesc: Structured intake and response process for employee accommodation needs. Ensures legally compliant, consistent, and person-centred responses across your organization.
NEURO.accomStep1Desc: Confidential intake form capturing the employee's needs, preferred accommodations, and any supporting documentation.
NEURO.accomStep2Desc: HR reviews the request within 5 business days. AI generates a draft accommodation plan for HR to review and adapt.
NEURO.accomStep3Desc: Accommodation plan is confirmed with the employee, manager, and HR — all parties receive a copy.
NEURO.accomStep4Desc: Automated follow-up survey to evaluate accommodation effectiveness and adjust as needed.
NEURO.trainingDesc: Microlearning content on supporting neurodivergent employees — practical, evidence-based, and designed for people managers without clinical backgrounds.
NEURO.trainAdhdDesc: Practical strategies for supporting employees with ADHD — task structure, deadline flexibility, and reducing distraction.
NEURO.trainAutismDesc: Sensory considerations, communication preferences, social expectations, and creating predictable environments.
NEURO.trainDyslexiaDesc: Reading and numeracy accommodations, alternative formats, assistive technology, and assessment adjustments.
NEURO.trainSensoryDesc: How to identify sensory triggers, adapt physical workspaces, and support employees with sensory processing differences.
NEURO.trainCommunicationDesc: Clear, direct communication; written vs. verbal preferences; meeting structure; and reducing ambiguity.
NEURO.trainExecFunctionDesc: Structuring work to support planning, prioritization, and task initiation — for managers and team leads.
NEURO.roleDescription: Select your current role to help us tailor the assessment to your perspective.
NEURO.rateMaturity: Rate your organization's maturity in this dimension

SUCCESSION.title: Leadership & Succession Hub™
SUCCESSION.subtitle: Scalable succession planning and leadership coaching — from founders and family businesses to growing organizations
SUCCESSION.methodology: Methodology: Transforms Helena's high-touch succession planning and leadership coaching into a scalable platform. Every IDP is structured around the proven GROW model (Goal → Reality → Options → Will Do) with EQi-informed competency mapping and AI-generated 90-day milestones.
SUCCESSION.scorecardDesc: Board-ready reporting on next-generation leadership bench strength. Share with executives and board members to demonstrate succession health at a glance.
SUCCESSION.noIDPsManage: Generate your first AI-powered IDP using the GROW model.
SUCCESSION.noIDPsView: No development plan has been created for you yet. Contact your coach or HR manager.
SUCCESSION.developmentGoalPlaceholder: e.g. Transition from individual contributor to people manager within 12 months
SUCCESSION.eqiScoresHint: Enter scores from 1–130. Leave at 100 if not assessed.
SUCCESSION.aiNote: Claude AI will generate a full GROW-model IDP with milestones. This takes about 10–15 seconds.
SUCCESSION.generateIDPFailed: Failed to generate IDP. Please try again.
SUCCESSION.regenerateIdpMessage: The current GROW content and milestones will be replaced with a new AI-generated plan.
SUCCESSION.deleteIdpMessage: Delete the development plan for {{name}}? This cannot be undone.
SUCCESSION.pickPrompt: Pick a prompt or write freely:
SUCCESSION.prompt1: What progress have you made toward your goal since your last session?
SUCCESSION.prompt2: What obstacles are you encountering — and what's really driving them?
SUCCESSION.prompt3: Which of your GROW options have you acted on, and what did you learn?
SUCCESSION.prompt4: What support do you need from your manager or mentor right now?
SUCCESSION.prompt5: On a scale of 1–10, how confident are you in achieving your 90-day milestone?
SUCCESSION.prompt6: What would 'success' look like at your next coaching session?
SUCCESSION.helenaIntegrationDesc: Book coaching sessions, EQi-2.0 debriefs, and stakeholder alignment workshops directly through the platform. Helena's availability is synced and sessions are linked to your active IDPs.
SUCCESSION.svcEqiDesc: Full emotional intelligence assessment with a 60-min debrief session. Results integrated directly into your IDP competency framework.
SUCCESSION.svcGrowDesc: Individual coaching session using the GROW model, aligned to your active IDP goals and milestones.
SUCCESSION.svcStakeholderDesc: Facilitated 90-min session with the coachee, manager, and HR to align on IDP goals, success criteria, and organizational support.
SUCCESSION.svcStrategyDesc: Full-day facilitated session with leadership team to map bench strength, identify critical roles, and build organizational succession strategy.

SURVEY.scoringMethodHint: Ipsative = fixed point total across subscales

SURVEY.subscaleConfigHint: Rich per-subscale metadata (CDP, WCQ). Keyed by subscale name.
SURVEY.benchmarksHint: Norm-referenced interpretive bands for coaching reports (PSS). Keyed by band name.

SURVEY.questionTextHint: For forced_choice: frames the behavioural dilemma; statements live in options below

SURVEY.scaleLabelsHint: Keys are numeric strings matching scale points, e.g. "1", "2", ...
SURVEY.referencePeriodHint: Temporal anchor for frequency-based items (WCQ, ROCI-II)
SURVEY.descriptionHint: A brief description shown to coaches and admins
SURVEY.instructionsHint: Shown to respondents before they begin. Supports basic formatting.

terms.s1p1: These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern your access to and use of the ARTES platform ("Platform", "Service"), operated by HeadSoft Technology in partnership with Helena Coaching ("we", "us", "our"). By accessing or using the Platform, you agree to be bound by these Terms.
terms.s1p2: ARTES is a multi-tenant B2B SaaS platform designed for organisational development, coaching, and people intelligence. The Platform is available at artes.helenacoaching.com.
terms.s2li1: "Organisation" means the entity that has subscribed to the Platform and manages its own users, departments, and data.
terms.s2li2: "User" means any individual granted access to the Platform by their Organisation, including administrators, HR managers, managers, coaches, and coachees.
terms.s2li3: "Content" means any data, text, responses, analyses, or materials submitted to or generated by the Platform.
terms.s2li4: "AI Features" means any functionality powered by artificial intelligence, including conflict analysis, neuroinclusion gap assessments, individual development plan generation, and coaching insights.
terms.s3p1: 3.1. Access to the Platform is provided on an Organisation basis. Your Organisation's administrator is responsible for managing user accounts and role assignments.
terms.s3p2: 3.2. You must provide accurate and complete information when your account is created. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your login credentials.
terms.s3p3: 3.3. The Platform supports the following roles with varying access levels: System Administrator, Organisation Administrator, HR Manager, Manager, Coach, and Coachee. Your access is governed by the role assigned to you.
terms.s3p4: 3.4. You must not share your credentials, attempt to access accounts belonging to others, or circumvent access controls.
terms.s4p1: You agree to use the Platform only for its intended purpose — organisational development, coaching, people analytics, and related professional activities. You must not:
terms.s4li1: Use the Platform for any unlawful purpose or in violation of any applicable laws or regulations.
terms.s4li2: Attempt to reverse-engineer, decompile, or extract source code from the Platform.
terms.s4li3: Interfere with the Platform's operation, security, or other users' access.
terms.s4li4: Submit false, misleading, or malicious content including in survey responses or coaching intake forms.
terms.s4li5: Use AI-generated analyses as the sole basis for employment decisions without human review.
terms.s4li6: Scrape, harvest, or collect data from the Platform by automated means.
terms.s5p1: 5.1. The Platform is offered through tiered subscription plans. Your Organisation's plan determines the features available and the maximum number of users.
terms.s5p2: 5.2. Subscription fees are billed at the Organisation level. Payment processing is handled securely through Stripe. We do not store credit card details on our servers.
terms.s5p3: 5.3. Subscriptions renew automatically unless cancelled before the renewal date. Your Organisation administrator can manage the subscription from the billing section of the Platform.
terms.s5p4: 5.4. We reserve the right to modify pricing with 30 days' written notice. Existing subscriptions will be honoured until the next renewal cycle.
terms.s6p1: 6.1. Your Data: Your Organisation retains full ownership of all data submitted to the Platform, including survey responses, coaching notes, assessment results, and organisational information. We claim no ownership over your data.
terms.s6p2: 6.2. AI-Generated Content: Analyses, narratives, scripts, and development plans generated by the Platform's AI features are provided to your Organisation for its use. These outputs are derived from your data and standard organisational development methodologies.
terms.s6p3: 6.3. Platform IP: The Platform software, design, algorithms, and underlying technology remain the intellectual property of HeadSoft Technology. Your subscription grants a non-exclusive, non-transferable licence to use the Platform for the subscription term.
terms.s6p4: 6.4. Feedback: If you provide feedback or suggestions about the Platform, we may use them to improve the service without obligation to you.
terms.s7p1: 7.1. The Platform uses AI (powered by Anthropic's Claude) to generate conflict analyses, neuroinclusion assessments, individual development plans, and coaching insights.
terms.s7p2: 7.2. AI-generated content is intended as a professional support tool, not a replacement for qualified human judgment. All AI outputs should be reviewed by appropriate professionals before action is taken.
terms.s7p3: 7.3. We do not guarantee the accuracy, completeness, or suitability of AI-generated content for any particular purpose. AI analyses are based on the data provided and standard organisational psychology frameworks.
terms.s7p4: 7.4. Your data is processed by the AI model for the sole purpose of generating the requested analysis. We do not use your data to train AI models.
terms.s8p1: 8.1. Your privacy is important to us. Our collection and processing of personal data is governed by our Privacy Statement.
terms.s8p2: 8.2. The Platform is designed with privacy-by-design principles, including data segregation between organisations, minimum group sizes for survey aggregation (5 respondents), and anonymous survey options.
terms.s8p3: 8.3. We act as a Data Processor on behalf of your Organisation (the Data Controller) for the purposes of GDPR and equivalent data protection legislation.
terms.s9p1: 9.1. The Platform integrates with third-party services including Google Calendar, Microsoft 365 (Outlook), Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, and Stripe. Your use of these integrations is subject to the respective third-party terms of service.
terms.s9p2: 9.2. Calendar integrations require explicit OAuth consent. Tokens are stored encrypted and can be revoked at any time from the Platform settings.
terms.s9p3: 9.3. We are not responsible for the availability, accuracy, or security of third-party services.
terms.s10p1: 10.1. We strive to maintain high availability of the Platform but do not guarantee uninterrupted access. The Platform may be temporarily unavailable for maintenance, updates, or due to circumstances beyond our control.
terms.s10p2: 10.2. We will make reasonable efforts to provide advance notice of planned maintenance that may affect availability.
terms.s11p1: 11.1. To the maximum extent permitted by law, HeadSoft Technology and Helena Coaching shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of or inability to use the Platform.
terms.s11p2: 11.2. Our total liability for any claims arising from the use of the Platform shall not exceed the total subscription fees paid by your Organisation in the 12 months preceding the claim.
terms.s11p3: 11.3. We do not accept liability for decisions made based on AI-generated content. The Platform is a decision-support tool, not a decision-making authority.
terms.s12p1: 12.1. Your Organisation may terminate its subscription at any time. Upon termination, access to the Platform will cease at the end of the current billing period.
terms.s12p2: 12.2. We may suspend or terminate access if you breach these Terms, fail to pay subscription fees, or engage in activity that threatens the Platform's security or other users.
terms.s12p3: 12.3. Upon termination, your Organisation may request an export of its data within 30 days. After this period, data will be permanently deleted in accordance with our data retention policy.
terms.s13p1: We may update these Terms from time to time. We will notify Organisation administrators of material changes at least 30 days before they take effect. Continued use of the Platform after changes take effect constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms.
terms.s14p1: These Terms are governed by the laws of the Czech Republic. Any disputes arising under these Terms shall be resolved in the competent courts of Prague, Czech Republic.
terms.s15p1: For questions about these Terms, please contact:

privacy.s1p1: HeadSoft Technology ("we", "us", "our") operates the ARTES platform in partnership with Helena Coaching. This Privacy Statement explains how we collect, use, store, and protect personal data when you use the ARTES platform ("Platform").
privacy.s1p2: We are committed to protecting your privacy and processing personal data in compliance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), the Czech Data Protection Act, and other applicable data protection legislation.
privacy.s2controller: Data Controller: Your Organisation (the entity that subscribed to ARTES) determines the purposes and means of processing personal data.
privacy.s2processor: Data Processor: HeadSoft Technology processes personal data on behalf of your Organisation, in accordance with a Data Processing Agreement.
privacy.s2p1: For personal data related to your ARTES account and platform usage, HeadSoft Technology acts as the Data Controller.
privacy.s3h1intro: When your Organisation creates your account, we collect:
privacy.s3h1li1: Full name, email address, department, and role within the organisation
privacy.s3h1li2: Hashed password (never stored in plain text)
privacy.s3h2li1: Responses to organisational surveys (conflict assessment, neuroinclusion, coaching intake)
privacy.s3h2li2: Anonymous survey responses are linked only by a submission token — we cannot identify the respondent
privacy.s3h2li3: Coach-led interview responses, including session format and target participant names
privacy.s3h3li1: Coaching engagement records (coach-coachee pairings, session history)
privacy.s3h3li2: Individual Development Plans (IDPs) including goals, milestones, and progress notes
privacy.s3h3li3: Session notes, journal entries, and pre/post-session form responses
privacy.s3h3li4: Booking information (session times, contact details, topics)
privacy.s3h4li1: Aggregated survey responses submitted for AI analysis (conflict risk, neuroinclusion gaps)
privacy.s3h4li2: AI-generated outputs: narratives, risk assessments, development plans, manager scripts
privacy.s3h5li1: IP address, browser type, device information (for security and access logging)
privacy.s3h5li3: Calendar OAuth tokens (Google Calendar, Microsoft 365) — stored encrypted
privacy.s4r1purpose: Providing the Platform and its features
privacy.s5p1: 5.1. The Platform uses Anthropic's Claude AI to generate analyses. Your data is sent to Anthropic's API for processing when you explicitly request an AI analysis.
privacy.s5p2: 5.2. We do not use your data to train AI models. Anthropic's API usage terms confirm that data submitted via the API is not used for model training.
privacy.s5p3: 5.3. AI processing is triggered only by explicit user action (e.g., clicking "Analyse" or "Generate IDP"). Data is not continuously or passively processed by AI.
privacy.s5p4: 5.4. AI-generated outputs are stored within your Organisation's data and subject to the same access controls and retention policies.
privacy.s6p1: We do not sell your personal data. We share data only with:
privacy.s6li1: Anthropic (Claude AI): Aggregated survey data and coaching context, only when AI analysis is explicitly requested.
privacy.s6li2: Amazon Web Services (AWS): Infrastructure hosting (EC2, SES for email). Data is processed within AWS's security framework.
privacy.s6li3: MongoDB Atlas: Database hosting with encryption at rest and in transit.
privacy.s6li4: Stripe: Payment processing. We do not store credit card numbers.
privacy.s6li5: Google / Microsoft: Calendar data, only when you explicitly connect your calendar via OAuth.
privacy.s7li1: Encryption: All data in transit is encrypted via TLS/HTTPS. Database connections use TLS. OAuth tokens are stored with select: false (excluded from default queries).
privacy.s7li2: Authentication: JWT tokens with 15-minute expiry and 7-day refresh tokens. Optional two-factor authentication (TOTP) and passkey/WebAuthn support.
privacy.s7li3: Multi-tenancy: Strict data segregation between organisations via organisationId filtering on every database query. A tenant filter plugin enforces this at the database layer.
privacy.s7li4: Access control: Role-based access control (RBAC) with six distinct role levels. Permissions are enforced both in the API and the user interface.
privacy.s7li5: Password security: Passwords are hashed using bcrypt. Plain-text passwords are never stored or logged.
privacy.s7li6: Rate limiting: API endpoints are rate-limited to prevent abuse.
privacy.s8p1: 8.1. Survey responses can be submitted anonymously via public links. Anonymous responses are linked only by an irreversible submission token.
privacy.s8p2: 8.2. AI analysis requires a minimum of 5 survey responses before aggregated results are generated. This prevents statistical de-anonymisation of individual respondents.
privacy.s8p3: 8.3. Conflict analyses, neuroinclusion assessments, and other AI outputs are based on aggregated data and do not identify individual respondents.
privacy.s9li1: Active accounts: Data is retained for the duration of the Organisation's subscription.
privacy.s9li2: After termination: Data is retained for 30 days to allow export, then permanently deleted.
privacy.s9li3: Authentication logs: Retained for 90 days for security purposes.
privacy.s9li4: Backup data: Encrypted backups are retained for 30 days, then purged.
privacy.s10p1: As a data subject, you have the right to:
privacy.s10li1: Access: Request a copy of your personal data held on the Platform.
privacy.s10li2: Rectification: Request correction of inaccurate personal data.
privacy.s10li3: Erasure: Request deletion of your personal data ("right to be forgotten").
privacy.s10li4: Restriction: Request limitation of processing in certain circumstances.
privacy.s10li5: Data portability: Receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format.
privacy.s10li6: Object: Object to processing based on legitimate interest.
privacy.s10li7: Withdraw consent: Withdraw consent for calendar integrations at any time via the Platform settings.
privacy.s10p2: To exercise these rights, contact your Organisation's administrator or email us directly. We will respond within 30 days.
privacy.s11p1: The Platform is hosted on AWS infrastructure. Data may be processed in regions where AWS and our sub-processors operate. Where data is transferred outside the European Economic Area, we ensure appropriate safeguards are in place, including Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs).
privacy.s12p1: The Platform uses only essential cookies and local storage for authentication (JWT tokens) and user preferences (language, timezone, UI settings). We do not use tracking cookies, analytics cookies, or advertising cookies.
privacy.s13p1: The Platform is designed for professional use and is not directed at individuals under 16 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children.
privacy.s14p1: We may update this Privacy Statement periodically. We will notify Organisation administrators of material changes at least 30 days before they take effect. The "Last updated" date above reflects the most recent revision.
privacy.s15p1: For privacy-related enquiries, data subject requests, or complaints:
privacy.s15authority: You also have the right to lodge a complaint with your local data protection authority. In the Czech Republic, this is the Office for Personal Data Protection (www.uoou.cz).

eula.importantBox: IMPORTANT — READ CAREFULLY: This End User Licence Agreement ("EULA") is a legal agreement between you ("User") and HeadSoft Technology ("Licensor") for the use of the ARTES software platform ("Software"). By accessing or using the Software, you agree to be bound by this EULA.
eula.s1p1: 1.1. Subject to the terms of this EULA and a valid subscription, the Licensor grants you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable licence to access and use the Software via a web browser for your Organisation's internal business purposes.
eula.s1p2: 1.2. This licence is granted on a per-Organisation basis. The number of authorised users is determined by your Organisation's subscription plan.
eula.s1p3: 1.3. The Software is provided as a hosted service (SaaS). No software is installed on your devices — access is via the internet at artes.helenacoaching.com.
eula.s2li1: Copy, modify, distribute, sell, lease, sublicence, or create derivative works based on the Software.
eula.s2li2: Reverse-engineer, decompile, disassemble, or attempt to derive the source code of the Software.
eula.s2li3: Remove, obscure, or alter any proprietary notices, labels, or marks on the Software.
eula.s2li4: Use the Software to build a competing product or service.
eula.s2li5: Access the Software through any automated means (bots, scrapers, crawlers) except through documented APIs.
eula.s2li6: Share login credentials or allow unauthorised third parties to access the Software through your account.
eula.s2li7: Attempt to circumvent usage limits, access controls, or security measures.
eula.s3p1: 3.1. The Software, including its architecture, design, source code, algorithms, user interface, documentation, and all associated intellectual property rights, is and remains the exclusive property of HeadSoft Technology.
eula.s3p2: 3.2. This EULA does not grant you any ownership rights in the Software. You are granted only the limited licence described in Section 1.
eula.s3p3: 3.3. The ARTES name, logo, and associated branding are trademarks of HeadSoft Technology and Helena Coaching. You may not use these marks without prior written consent.
eula.s4p1: 4.1. You and your Organisation retain ownership of all data entered into the Software ("User Content"), including survey responses, coaching notes, assessment results, and organisational information.
eula.s4p2: 4.2. You grant the Licensor a limited licence to process User Content solely for the purpose of providing and improving the Software's functionality, including AI-powered analyses.
eula.s4p3: 4.3. AI-generated outputs (conflict analyses, development plans, neuroinclusion assessments) are derived from User Content using the Licensor's proprietary methodologies. These outputs are provided to your Organisation under the terms of this licence.
eula.s5p1: 5.1. The Software incorporates artificial intelligence features powered by third-party AI models. These features generate analytical outputs based on submitted data.
eula.s5p2: 5.2. AI-generated content is provided "as is" as a decision-support tool. It does not constitute professional advice in psychology, human resources, law, or any other domain.
eula.s5p3: 5.3. You acknowledge that AI outputs may contain inaccuracies and should be reviewed by qualified professionals before any action is taken.
eula.s5p4: 5.4. The Licensor does not warrant that AI-generated content will be accurate, complete, or fit for any particular purpose.
eula.s6p1: 6.1. The Software integrates with third-party services including:
eula.s6li1: Anthropic (Claude AI) — for AI-powered analysis and content generation
eula.s6li2: Google Calendar / Microsoft 365 — for calendar synchronisation and booking management
eula.s6li4: Amazon Web Services (AWS) — for infrastructure and email delivery (SES)
eula.s6p2: 6.2. Your use of these integrations may be subject to the respective third-party terms. The Licensor is not responsible for the availability, performance, or security of third-party services.
eula.s7p1: 7.1. The Licensor will make commercially reasonable efforts to maintain the Software's availability but does not guarantee uninterrupted access.
eula.s7p2: 7.2. The Licensor may update, modify, or enhance the Software at any time. Such updates may include bug fixes, feature additions, security patches, or changes to the user interface.
eula.s7p3: 7.3. The Licensor reserves the right to deprecate or remove features with reasonable notice to affected Organisations.
eula.s8box1: THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND "AS AVAILABLE" WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EITHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT.
eula.s8box2: THE LICENSOR DOES NOT WARRANT THAT THE SOFTWARE WILL MEET YOUR REQUIREMENTS, OPERATE WITHOUT INTERRUPTION, OR BE ERROR-FREE.
eula.s9p1: 9.1. To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, the Licensor shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of profits, data, or business opportunities, arising from your use of or inability to use the Software.
eula.s9p2: 9.2. The Licensor's total aggregate liability under this EULA shall not exceed the fees paid by your Organisation in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.
eula.s9p3: 9.3. The Licensor accepts no liability for decisions or actions taken based on AI-generated content.
eula.s10p1: 10.1. This EULA is effective from the date you first access the Software and continues for the duration of your Organisation's subscription.
eula.s10p2: 10.2. The Licensor may terminate this EULA immediately if you breach any of its terms.
eula.s10p3: 10.3. Upon termination, your right to access the Software ceases immediately. Sections 3, 8, 9, and 12 survive termination.
eula.s10p4: 10.4. Your Organisation may request data export within 30 days of termination. After this period, data will be permanently deleted.
eula.s11p1: The collection and processing of personal data through the Software is governed by our Privacy Statement. By using the Software, you acknowledge that you have read and understood the Privacy Statement.
eula.s12p1: 12.1. This EULA is governed by the laws of the Czech Republic, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.
eula.s12p2: 12.2. Any disputes arising under this EULA shall first be attempted to be resolved through good-faith negotiation. If negotiation fails, disputes shall be submitted to the competent courts of Prague, Czech Republic.
eula.s13p1: If any provision of this EULA is found to be unenforceable or invalid, that provision shall be limited or eliminated to the minimum extent necessary, and the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.
eula.s14p1: This EULA, together with the Terms of Service and Privacy Statement, constitutes the entire agreement between you and the Licensor regarding the use of the Software, and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings.

JOURNAL.coacheeMainTopicLabel: What is the main topic or challenge you want to explore in this session?
JOURNAL.coacheeMainTopicPlaceholder: Be as specific as possible — the clearer the focus, the more useful the session...
JOURNAL.coacheeValueDefinitionLabel: What would make this session feel truly valuable to you?
JOURNAL.coacheeValueDefinitionPlaceholder: What outcome, insight, or clarity would feel like success?
JOURNAL.coacheeRecentShiftsLabel: What has shifted or happened since your last session? (if applicable)
JOURNAL.coacheeContextForCoachLabel: Is there anything you'd like your coach to know before you begin?
JOURNAL.coacheeContextForCoachPlaceholder: Context, boundaries, or anything on your mind...
JOURNAL.coacheeDuringHint: Read-only. Notes your coach made during the session.
JOURNAL.coacheePostHint: Filled by you after the session. Your coach can read this.
JOURNAL.coacheeBiggestInsightLabel: What was your biggest insight or "aha" moment from this session?
JOURNAL.coacheeBiggestInsightPlaceholder: What landed most powerfully for you?
JOURNAL.coacheeWhatShiftedLabel: What feels different, clearer, or shifted after today's conversation?
JOURNAL.coacheeWhatShiftedPlaceholder: A new perspective, a decision made, something released...
JOURNAL.coacheeConfidenceLabel: How confident are you that you will follow through on these commitments?
JOURNAL.coacheeSessionRatingLabel: How would you rate the overall value of this session?
JOURNAL.coacheeExploreNextLabel: Is there anything you'd like to explore further in your next session?
JOURNAL.coacheeExploreNextPlaceholder: Themes to continue, questions that came up, topics to deepen...
JOURNAL.coacheeFeedbackForCoachLabel: Any other feedback or reflections for your coach?
JOURNAL.coacheeFeedbackForCoachPlaceholder: What worked well? What could be different?
